name: Chapter 2: Wilderness Random Encounters
description:
Random encounter table for Chapter 2 wilderness travel in Icewind Dale. Sourced from RotF pg. 105 and the Arctic Encounters (5e 2014) supplement by Duncan Thomson. Roll 1d20; results 9–13 are weighted to appear more often via range width. Reroll a 1 or treat as 11.

Threat Ratings: Low (CR 1/4–1), Moderate (CR 2–5), Dangerous (CR 6–10), Deadly (CR 11+).

results.text: Reroll / Crevasse — The party nearly steps into a hidden snow-covered crevasse (DC 13 Perception or fall 2d6×10 ft). Treat as 11 if preferred. | Hazard — no CR
Young Remorhaz — A juvenile remorhaz erupts from beneath the snow, drawn by the vibrations of the party's movement. It attacks the nearest creature relentlessly. | CR 5 — Dangerous

Frost Giant & Ice Mephits — A frost giant trudges through the tundra trailed by 1d4+2 ice mephits that swirl around its shoulders like living snowflakes. The giant ignores small creatures unless provoked. | CR 8 (giant) / CR 1/2 (mephits) — Deadly
Abominable Yeti — The massive white silhouette of an abominable yeti emerges from the blizzard wall. Its paralyzing gaze sweeps toward the party. | CR 9 — Deadly

Reghed Nomads — 2d6 Reghed tribal warriors on reindeer-drawn sleds cross the party's path. They may be hostile (Elk Tribe) or wary but tradeable (Bear/Wolf Tribe). Roll disposition: 1–2 hostile, 3–4 suspicious, 5–6 cautious neutral. | CR 1/8 each — Low

Polar Bear (hunting) — 1d2 polar bears are aggressively hunting. If the party has food smells or makes noise, the bears charge. A druid or ranger may attempt to calm one (DC 15 Animal Handling). | CR 2 — Moderate
Wolves — A pack of 3d6 wolves follows the party at distance for 1d4 hours before closing in, emboldened by hunger. If a wolf is killed, the rest retreat. | CR 1/4 each — Low to Moderate
Ice Mephits — 2d4 ice mephits cavort in a swirling snow devil, pelting travelers with sleet and shards. They flee if more than half are destroyed, cackling. | CR 1/2 each — Low

Orcs of the Many-Arrows — 1d4+2 orcs led by an orc eye of Gruumsh patrol a scavenging route, looking for survivors or loot from the eternal night. They attack on sight unless the party outnumbers them visibly. | CR 1/2 (orcs) / CR 2 (eye) — Moderate

Reindeer Herd — 3d6 reindeer (elk stats) cross a frozen lake, spooked by something behind them. DC 12 Perception reveals 1d2 winter wolves shadowing the herd's flank. | CR 1/4 (elk) / CR 3 (winter wolves) — Low to Moderate
Yetis — 1d3 yetis have been shadowing the party since their last campsite. They charge when the party crosses open ground with no cover. | CR 3 each — Moderate

Winter Wolves — 1d3 winter wolves fan out to flank, their breath crystallizing in the air. They coordinate intelligently, herding prey toward a snowdrift or ice shelf. | CR 3 each — Moderate to Dangerous
Saber-Toothed Tiger — A massive white-furred saber-toothed tiger crouches on a ridge, watching from above. It pounces on the smallest or most isolated party member. | CR 2 — Moderate
Gnolls — 2d4 gnolls from a wandering pack cackle as they crest a snowbank, already bloodied from a recent kill. They attack in a frenzy. | CR 1/2 each — Low to Moderate

Revenant — A figure in frozen traveler's clothes stands motionless on the trail. This revenant was betrayed by a party member it is now hunting — or so it believes. It ignores everyone else unless attacked. | CR 5 — Dangerous
Mammoth — A lone mammoth, maddened by hunger and Auril's endless winter, charges anything that crosses its territory. Tusks sweep 10-foot arcs. | CR 6 — Dangerous
Axe Beaks — 2d4 axe beaks race single-file across the tundra in a migratory column. They are panicked rather than aggressive — but will slash anything that blocks their path. | CR 1/4 each — Low
White Dragon Wyrmling — A white dragon wyrmling circles overhead, practicing its breath. It may offer a cruel bargain (tribute for passage) before attacking. | CR 2 — Moderate

Werebear (Lone Guardian) — A werebear in hybrid form blocks the trail, arms spread. It is territorial but not immediately hostile. It tests the party's intent, driving off those who seem dangerous. May become an ally if the party aids it or respects its territory. | CR 5 — Dangerous (but potentially neutral)
Remorhaz — A full-grown remorhaz erupts from below the snow, its heat-radiating carapace melting a crater around it. It swallows a creature whole on a hit of 20+. Flee or fight. | CR 11 — Deadly
